name: Bok Choy

supplementary_research.finding: Cruciferous vegetables including bok choy are associated with reduced all-cause mortality and lower risk of cardiovascular disease; meta-analysis of prospective cohort studies showed each daily serving reduces CVD risk by ~4%

Glucosinolates in bok choy (sinigrin, glucobrassicin) are hydrolyzed to isothiocyanates and indole-3-carbinol by myrosinase upon cell damage; these compounds upregulate Nrf2-mediated phase II detoxification enzymes and have demonstrated anti-tumor activity in vitro and in vivo

Sulforaphane (present in bok choy and other Brassica) activates AMPK and inhibits mTOR, pathways central to longevity; also inhibits NF-κB inflammatory signaling and reduces senescent cell burden in aged mouse models

Bok choy provides ~46 mg vitamin C per 100 g raw, 156 mcg folate, 89 mg calcium, and is an exceptional source of vitamin K1 (~46 mcg/100 g); low caloric density (~13 kcal/100 g) makes it an ideal high-volume, nutrient-dense food for longevity diets

Dietary indole-3-carbinol and diindolylmethane (derived from bok choy glucosinolates) modulate estrogen metabolism and exhibit protective effects against hormone-dependent cancers; also demonstrated extension of C. elegans lifespan via DAF-16/FOXO pathway activation

seasonality: Year-round; peak autumn–spring; baby bok choy available most months

nutrient_highlights.bioavailability_notes: Hydrolyzed to bioactive isothiocyanates and indole-3-carbinol by myrosinase; chopping or lightly chewing raw maximizes conversion; boiling destroys myrosinase but gut bacteria provide partial conversion
High bioavailability (~80%); water-soluble, sensitive to heat — lightly stir-fry or steam to preserve; synergistic with iron absorption
Fat-soluble; cook or dress with oil (sesame, olive) to enhance absorption; important for bone health and vascular calcification prevention
Bioavailability ~50–60%; supports DNA methylation and homocysteine regulation; pairs well with B12 sources for cardiovascular protection
Higher bioavailability (~54%) than dairy calcium; low oxalate content in bok choy unlike spinach makes it an excellent non-dairy calcium source
Fat-soluble pro-vitamin A; absorption enhanced by cooking with oil; concentrated in outer green leaves vs. white stalks

synergies.description: Classic Chinese pairing; ginger's gingerols complement bok choy's anti-inflammatory glucosinolate metabolites, and ginger aids digestion of the sulfur compounds

Garlic allicin and bok choy isothiocyanates both activate Nrf2 and NF-κB pathways synergistically; combined cruciferous-allium intake associated with greater cancer risk reduction than either alone (PMID 17317681)
Fat enhances absorption of fat-soluble vitamins K1 and beta-carotene; sesame lignans (sesamin, sesamolin) add independent antioxidant activity
Complete plant-based meal: bok choy provides calcium, vitamins K and C; tofu provides protein and isoflavones; traditional Japanese and Chinese longevity food combination
Salmon omega-3s enhance fat-soluble nutrient absorption from bok choy; vitamin D from salmon works synergistically with bok choy calcium for bone health
